title: HTTP request metrics with bounded label cardinality

difficulty.rationale: Requires correct route templating to bound label cardinality (the classic high-cardinality bug of labeling by raw id), excluding the metrics endpoint from its own counters, echo-vs-generate request-id logic, and emitting valid parseable Prometheus exposition text.

gates.name: security_baseline
gates.requirement: no hidden test access or network egress

must: Every response includes an X-Request-Id header.
http_requests_total is keyed by (method, route template, status).
Duration count and sum increase with each handled request.
must_not: Record GET /metrics in http_requests_total or the duration metrics.
Create a separate counter series per raw id value.
Drop the X-Request-Id header from any response.
edge_cases: GET /items/1 and GET /items/2 share a single route="/items/:id" series.
A 500 from GET /work?fail=1 is counted separately from 200s.
An empty or whitespace X-Request-Id is treated as absent and generated.

reward_model.description: Harbor reward.txt is computed by the bun-bench gate model in tests/test.sh.
